subject: Geography
title: The Ethiopian Highlands
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Ethiopian Highlands.

hook.text: The Ethiopian Highlands are called 'Africa's Water Tower' because they feed the Blue Nile.

buildup.text: They rise above 4,000 meters, creating a cool, green island in tropical East Africa.

discovery.text: Coffee was discovered here. Legend says a goatherd noticed his goats dancing after eating berries.

twist.text: These highlands isolated Ethiopia so effectively that it was never colonized by Europe.

climax.text: The rare Ethiopian wolf survives only here—the world's most endangered canine.

punchline.text: Africa's water tower also gave the world coffee.

quiz.question: Why are the Ethiopian Highlands called 'Africa's Water Tower'?
options: They have the most rainfall in Africa
